title: Highest temperature in Helsinki on June 15?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Helsinki Vantaa Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Helsinki Vantaa Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fi/vantaa/EFHK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the Finnish Meteorological Institute and other agencies converge on a 17°C high for Helsinki on June 15 under easterly winds, scattered showers, and extensive cloud cover that suppresses solar heating and caps boundary-layer warming. This narrow range reflects typical mid-June climatology modified by stable atmospheric conditions, with recent model runs showing minimal upward bias even if convection stays limited. Trader consensus at 100% on 17°C aligns with this tight guidance and observed temperatures remaining near or below that threshold. Clearer skies or delayed shower development could allow a brief spike toward 18°C, though current observations and ensemble spread make such shifts improbable before resolution.
